data.title: Aptose Biosciences Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Aptose Biosciences Inc., a clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on developing personalized therapies for the treatment of cancer, has released its annual Form 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces as it advances its lead compounds, tuspetinib and luxeptinib, through clinical trials.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenues: $0 million. The company did not generate any revenues for the years ended December 31, 2024, and 2023.
  • Net Loss: $25.4 million. The net loss decreased by approximately $25.8 million compared to the previous year, primarily due to a decrease in research and development program costs and personnel expenses.
  • Basic and Diluted Loss Per Common Share: $36.38. The loss per share decreased significantly from the previous year, reflecting the reduced net loss.

Business Highlights

  • Tuspetinib Development: Aptose is advancing tuspetinib, a lead program, for frontline combination therapy in newly diagnosed acute myeloid leukemia (AML) patients. The company is conducting a Phase 1/2 clinical trial to develop tuspetinib in combination with venetoclax and a hypomethylating agent (TUS+VEN+HMA) to create a superior triplet therapy for newly diagnosed AML patients.
  • Clinical Trial Progress: The company has completed a dose escalation and dose exploration international Phase 1/2 clinical trial for tuspetinib as a single agent in relapsed or refractory (R/R) AML patients. The trial demonstrated significant bone marrow blast reductions and clinical responses without dose-limiting toxicities across diverse AML populations.
  • Safety and Efficacy: Tuspetinib has shown a favorable safety profile with no drug-related QTc prolongations, liver or kidney toxicities, muscle damage, or differentiation syndrome. The TUS+VEN doublet combination therapy also maintained a favorable safety profile and achieved significant clinical responses in heavily pretreated R/R AML patients.
  • Luxeptinib Program: Luxeptinib, another oral kinase inhibitor, has demonstrated clinical activity in R/R AML and B-cell cancer patients. However, due to absorption issues with the original formulation, a new G3 formulation was developed, achieving better absorption and tolerability. The company is exploring alternative development paths and collaborations for luxeptinib.
  • Orphan Drug Designation: The FDA granted orphan drug designation to tuspetinib for the treatment of AML, providing benefits such as research and development tax credits, exemption from FDA application fees, and seven additional years of marketing exclusivity.
  • Collaborations and Agreements: Aptose entered into a Cooperative Research and Development Agreement (CRADA) with the National Cancer Institute (NCI) to collaborate on the clinical development of tuspetinib in the NCI Cancer Therapy Evaluation Program (CTEP) sponsored myeloMATCH trials.
  • Manufacturing and Supply: Following the Tuspetinib Licensing Agreement with Hanmi, Aptose received an existing inventory of drug product to support the current Phase 1/2 study and entered into a separate supply agreement for additional production to support further clinical development.
  • Future Outlook: Aptose plans to initiate the TUS+VEN+AZA triplet study in newly diagnosed AML patients, with a focus on establishing a new standard of care that increases response rates, extends survival, and improves quality of life for genetically diverse AML patient populations.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Focus: Aptose Biosciences Inc. is focused on advancing its lead compound, tuspetinib, through clinical trials, including a Phase 1/2 trial for a triplet therapy in newly diagnosed AML patients. The company has entered into a Cooperative Research and Development Agreement with the National Cancer Institute to further develop tuspetinib in precision medicine trials. Additionally, Aptose is exploring alternative development paths and collaborations for its other compound, luxeptinib, while pausing its funding due to prioritization of tuspetinib.
  • Capital Management: The company has engaged in several capital management activities, including a 1-for-30 reverse stock split to regain compliance with Nasdaq's minimum bid price requirement. Aptose has utilized various financing mechanisms such as a committed equity facility with Keystone Capital Partners, a public offering, and a private placement with Hanmi Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd. These efforts have raised significant funds to support ongoing operations and clinical trials. The company has also addressed Nasdaq compliance issues related to shareholder equity and private placement rules.
  • Future Outlook: Aptose plans to continue its strategic focus on developing tuspetinib as a potential new standard of care for AML, while seeking additional financing to support its operations beyond April 2025. The company aims to explore further collaborations and partnerships to enhance its capital position and advance its drug development programs. Management is considering various options for raising capital, including equity financing, debt, and strategic partnerships, to ensure the continuation of its research and development activities.

Challenges and Risks

  • Going Concern: The company faces substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern beyond April 2025, necessitating additional financing to meet capital requirements. The company is exploring strategies such as equity financing and operational restructuring to secure necessary funding, but challenges in financial markets may impede access to capital.
  • Early-Stage Development: The company is an early-stage development entity with no revenue from product sales, and its product candidates require significant funding to reach regulatory approval. The company has a history of operating losses and expects to incur net losses for the foreseeable future, with no assurance of achieving profitability.
  • Key Personnel: The company heavily relies on key executives and scientists, and the loss of any could impact product development. Employee misconduct, including noncompliance with regulatory standards, poses a risk of material adverse effects.
  • Sales and Marketing: The company lacks sales, marketing, or distribution experience, necessitating significant investment to establish these capabilities or reliance on third parties.
  • Expansion Risks: The company may expand through acquisitions or collaborations, which could disrupt business and harm financial condition. Fluctuations in exchange rates, particularly between the U.S. and Canadian dollars, could result in currency exchange losses, impacting financial results.
  • Clinical Trials and Manufacturing: The company’s product candidates, tuspetinib and luxeptinib, are in early clinical trial phases, requiring significant funding for further development. Delays in clinical testing could harm business prospects, and the company relies on contract manufacturers, which poses risks of quality, cost, or delivery issues. The company is exposed to international trade risks due to reliance on foreign manufacturers.
  • Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks, including foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, which may adversely affect financial results. The company does not currently employ a hedging strategy against exchange rate risk, which could lead to material impacts on financial statements and distort period-to-period comparisons.

SEC Filing: Aptose Biosciences Inc. [ APTO ] - 10-K - Mar. 28, 2025

data.title: Mob kills seven suspected kidnappers in Nigeria's Edo state
data.description:
At least seven suspected kidnappers were set ablaze and killed in a "barbaric" mob attack in Nigeria's southern town of Uromi, in Edo state, the governor said on Friday.

Edo Governor Monday Okpebholo condemned the mob killing of the individuals, pledging that those responsible would be prosecuted to the fullest extent of the law.

Preliminary investigations revealed that the victims, identified as northern Hausa hunters, were stopped by local security while travelling in a commercial truck.

A search of the vehicle uncovered guns, triggering a mob attack by residents who accused them of being kidnappers and Fulani herdsmen.

"The commissioner of police has been directed to launch a full scale investigation into the matter," Okpebholo said in a statement, adding that at least four people have been arrested for their involvement in the attack.

Kidnapping for ransom and violence attributed to Fulani herders have become a pervasive and escalating crisis in Nigeria, fuelling insecurity, displacement, and inter-ethnic tensions across the country.

Hundreds have been killed in recent years in clashes pitting the semi-nomadic, cattle-herding Fulani people against more settled farming communities in Nigeria.

data.title: TD SYNNEX's Q1 Billings Growth Despite Weaker Hyve Segment Demand Reason to Remain Buyers, RBC Says
data.description:
TD SYNNEX's Q1 "solid billings growth" of 7.5% surpassed market expectations, despite weaker demand in its Hyve segment, which is why "we believe secular tailwinds remain in place over the mid-term and would remain buyers on weakness," RBC Capital Markets said in a note to clients Friday.

TD SYNNEX achieved an operating profit of $399 million and a higher-than-expected gross margin of 6.87% as a percent of sales, indicating good cost control, RBC said.

Tariffs had minimal impact on TD SYNNEX, due to its US manufacturing base and flexible supply chain, giving Hyve a market advantage, RBC added.

RBC maintained an outperform rating for TD SYNNEX with a price target of $165.

data.title: Kyndryl's Weakness Provides Buying Opportunity, Oppenheimer Says
data.description:
Kyndryl's weakness following a short report is seen as an opportunity to buy and add to positions, Oppenheimer said in a note Friday.

Oppenheimer analysts said they remain bullish on the stock and believed the company's accounting and disclosures "do not suggest nefarious activity."

The lower related party cost of services in fiscal year 2023, as highlighted in the short report released Thursday, is explained by the fact that IBM is no longer a related party as it sold down its post-spinoff ownership in Kyndryl in August 2022, the note said.

The analysts also said they do not see a meaningful attempt to manipulate free cash flow. An unknown increase in cost of service will also likely not materialize, as claimed by the short report, according to the note.

"Overall, we do not believe there is a 'smoking gun,'" Oppenheimer said, adding that management has said the short report contained "inaccurate and deliberately misleading" claims.

Oppenheimer reiterated its stock rating at outperform, with a price target of $43.

data.title: REG - Velocity Composites - Exercise of Share Options and Issue of Equity
data.description:
RNS Number : 7571C Velocity Composites PLC 28 March 2025  

28 March 2025

Velocity Composites plc

("Velocity" or the "Company")

Exercise of Share Options and Issue of Equity

Velocity Composites plc (AIM: VEL.L), the leading supplier of advanced composite material kits to the aerospace market, announces that on 28 March 2025, 17,000 options over Velocity ordinary shares of 0.25 pence each ("Ordinary Shares") were exercised by a member of staff at a price of 0.24p.

Application has been made for 17,000 Ordinary Shares to be admitted to trading on AIM ("Admission"). Admission is expected to occur on 3 April 2025 and the Ordinary Shares will, on issue, rank pari passu in all respects with the Company's existing Ordinary Shares.

On Admission, the Company will have a total of 54,010,366 Ordinary Shares in issue.  No Ordinary Shares are held in treasury.  The figure of 54,010,366 may be used by the Company's shareholders as the denominator in the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure, Guidance and Transparency Rules.

About Velocity Composites plc

Based in Burnley, UK, Velocity is the leading supplier of composite material kits to aerospace and other high-performance manufacturers, that reduce costs and improve sustainability.  Customers include Airbus, Boeing, and GKN.

By using Velocity's proprietary technology, manufacturers can also free up internal resources to focus on their core business.  Velocity has significant potential for expansion, both in the UK and abroad, including into new market areas, such as wind energy, urban air mobility and electric vehicles, where the demand for composites is expected to grow.

data.title: Merus Price Target Maintained With a $109.00/Share by Guggenheim
Lululemon Shares Stumble as Inflation Softens U.S. Demand, Rivals Gain Ground
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese

Shares in lululemon athletica slipped in early trading Friday after the company said it faces cooling demand in its core U.S. market as inflation and economic uncertainty intensify competition.

Shares fell over 15% to $289.12 in morning trading.

The Vancouver, British Columbia-based athleticwear brand, which generates the bulk of its revenue in the U.S., said late Thursday that shoppers are pulling back in the first quarter, a trend that management thinks could persist throughout the fiscal year.

"We expected more robust U.S. growth and a return to positive comps in 2025 after last year's product missteps, but we no longer have high confidence on this outcome," Raymond James analyst Rick Patel said in a report. The firm downgraded the stock rating to market perform from a previous outperform rating.

For the current year, lululemon expects revenue between $11.15 billion and $11.3 billion--roughly in line with expectations, according to FactSet--but its per-share earnings guidance of $14.95 to $15.15 missed the $15.37 that analysts expected.

On Thursday's earnings call, Chief Executive Calvin McDonald said lululemon has rolled out several new product launches to bolster its collection of yoga apparel and other athletic gear, all of which have been well received by shoppers, but he said this may not be enough to counter softer demand.

"We also believe the dynamic macro environment has contributed to a more cautious consumer," McDonald said, citing a survey it did with Ipsos, as consumers spend less due to mounting concerns about inflation and the economy. He noted that this is manifesting itself into slower traffic in the U.S. both for the company and for the industry in general.

The company guided for a slight climb in the U.S. "We are controlling what we can control, and we expect to see modest growth in U.S. revenue for the full year of 2025," he said.

Raymond James' Patel said the theme is presenting itself industry-wide, but that lululemon faces the added challenge of newcomers on the scene, namely Alo and Vuori.

"We've seen this theme called out by several companies over the last couple of months, but we believe LULU has the added challenge of strong emerging competitors," Patel said.

The dual effects of slowing consumer demand and added pressure are already being felt in the first quarter.

Lululemon is guiding for first-quarter earnings of $2.53 to $2.58 per share on revenue of $2.34 billion to $2.36 billion--shy of analyst expectations, which called for earnings of $2.72 a share on sales of $2.39 billion.

Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com

data.title: Johnson Fistel has Commenced an Investigation on Behalf of Proficient Auto Logistics, Inc. (PAL) Shareholders
data.description:
SAN DIEGO, March 28, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Shareholder rights law firm Johnson Fistel, PLLP is investigating whether Proficient Auto Logistics, Inc. or any of its executive officers or others violated securities laws by misrepresenting or failing to timely disclose material information to investors. The investigation focuses on investors’ losses and whether they may be recovered under federal securities laws.

What if I purchased PAL securities? If you purchased securities and suffered losses on your investment, join our investigation now:

Click Here to Join the Investigation

Or for more information, contact Jim Baker at jimb@johnsonfistel.com or (619) 814-4471.

There is no cost or obligation to you.

What is Johnson Fistel Investigating? On October 16, 2024, Proficient Auto Logistics issued a report pertaining to anticipated operating and financial metrics for the third quarter of fiscal year 2024.

The Company stated that they estimated revenue for the third quarter to be in the range of $90 to $92 million, reflecting a decrease of 14 to 16% relative to the combined revenue of the third quarter of 2023.

Following this announcement, the Company's stock experienced a decline of 28.77% in the subsequent trading session.

What if I have relevant nonpublic information? Individuals with nonpublic information regarding the company should consider whether to assist our investigation or take advantage of the SEC Whistleblower program. Under the SEC program, whistleblowers who provide original information may, under certain circumstances, receive rewards totaling up to thirty percent of any successful recovery made by the SEC. For more information, contact Jim Baker at (619) 814-4471 or jimb@johnsonfistel.com.

About Johnson Fistel, PLLP | Top Law Firm, Securities Fraud, Investors Rights:

Johnson Fistel, PLLP is a nationally recognized shareholder rights law firm with offices in California, New York, Georgia, Idaho, and Colorado. The firm represents individual and institutional investors in shareholder derivative and securities class action lawsuits. We also extend our services to foreign investors who have purchased on US exchanges. Stay updated with news on stock drops and learn how Johnson Fistel, PLLP can help you recover your losses. For more information about the firm and its attorneys, please visit http://www.johnsonfistel.com.

Achievements: In 2024, Johnson Fistel was honored to be ranked in the Top 10 Plaintiff Law Firms by the ISS Securities Class Action Services. This recognition underscores our effectiveness in advocating for investors, having recovered approximately $90,725,000 for aggrieved clients in cases where we served as lead or co-lead counsel. This notable accomplishment marks the eighth occasion our firm has been recognized as a top plaintiffs’ securities law firm in the United States, as determined by the total dollar value of final recoveries.

Attorney advertising.

Past results do not guarantee future outcomes.

Services may be performed by attorneys in any of our offices.

Johnson Fistel, PLLP has paid for the dissemination of this promotional communication, and Frank J. Johnson is the attorney responsible for its content.

Contact:

Johnson Fistel, PLLP

501 W. Broadway, Suite 800, San Diego, CA 92101

James Baker, Investor Relations or Frank J. Johnson, Esq., (619) 814-4471

data.title: Fusion Fuel Green Launches 6.2 Million Shares Offering on Behalf of Keystone Capital Partners
data.description:
Fusion Fuel Green said in a regulatory filing Friday it has launched an offering on behalf of selling shareholder Keystone Capital Partners to sell up to approximately 6.2 million class A ordinary shares of the company.

The company will not receive any proceeds from the offering and may get up to $25 million in total gross proceeds under the purchase agreement it signed with the selling stockholder of up to about 5.8 million of its class A ordinary shares, Fusion Fuel said in a Form F-3 filed with the US Securities and Exchange Commission.

Fusion Fuel shares were up 2.5% in recent Friday trading.

data.title: Stocks drop more than 1% early as investors digest data
data.description:
Key points:
  • All three major US stock indexes off >1%; Nasdaq down 1.9%
  • Cons. disc off most among S&P 500 sectors; utilities sole gainer
  • Euro STOXX 600 index off ~0.9%
  • Dollar, crude dip; gold up; bitcoin down >3%
  • U.S. 10-Year Treasury yield falls to ~4.28%

STOCKS DROP MORE THAN 1% EARLY AS INVESTORS DIGEST DATA

All three major U.S. stock indexes were selling off more than 1% in early trading Friday as investors digested U.S. data showing consumer spending rebounded in February amid rising prices for goods and services.

Consumer spending, which accounts for more than two-thirds of economic activity, climbed 0.4% after a downwardly revised 0.3% decline in January, the Commerce Department's Bureau of Economic Analysis said.

The Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) price index increased 0.3% in February after advancing by the same unrevised margin in January and in line with economists' expectations.

"There's a blessing and a curse - consumer spending held up, but inflation came in a little hotter. The market looks a little 'sad' but not terribly sad... inflation was hotter than expected but it didn't come in terribly hot," said Kim Forrest, chief investment officer at Bokeh Capital Partners in Pittsburgh.

"I don't think we can say it's 'killed' necessarily, but it's not quite enough to make people stop spending. I don't think we'll see bad things happen in the stock market today."

Investors have been worried about rising inflation given myriad tariff announcements by the Trump administration and an escalating trade war.

The consumer discretionary index led declines among sectors.

Stocks ended lower on Thursday, with shares of auto makers down after U.S. President Donald Trump announced late Wednesday his plan to implement 25% tariffs on imported cars and light trucks effective next week.

Here is the early market snapshot:

(Caroline Valetkevitch, Lisa Mattackal)

****

FOR FRIDAY'S EARLIER LIVE MARKETS POSTS:

INDIVIDUAL INVESTOR BEARS HAVE BEEN ON QUITE A RUN - AAII

Notes to Editors

Chesnara (CSN.L) is a European life and pensions consolidator listed on the London Stock Exchange.  It administers under one million policies and operates as Countrywide Assured in the UK, as The Waard Group and Scildon in the Netherlands and as Movestic in Sweden.

Following a three-pillar strategy, Chesnara's primary responsibility is the efficient administration of its customers' life and savings policies, ensuring good customer outcomes and providing a secure and compliant environment to protect policyholder interests. It also adds value by writing profitable new business in the UK, Sweden and the Netherlands and by undertaking value-adding acquisitions of either companies or portfolios.

Consistent delivery of the Company strategy has enabled Chesnara to increase its dividend for 20 years in succession.

Further details are available on the Company's website (www.chesnara.co.uk).

data.description:

Financial details weren't disclosed.

data.title: Bosnia’s stock markets close mixed in week ended Mar 28
data.description:
SARAJEVO (Bosnia and Herzegovina), March 28 (SeeNews) - The equity indices of Bosnia and Herzegovina's Sarajevo Stock Exchange (SASE) and the Banja Luka Stock Exchange (BLSE) recorded mixed performances in the week ending March 28, bourse data showed.

Total turnover on the SASE dropped to 53.7 million marka ($29.7 million/27.5 million euro) this week from 111.4 million marka a week earlier.

The SASE's BIFX, which monitors investment funds, lost 0.81% week-on-week to close at 510.60 points on Friday.

The bourse's main equity index SASX-10 index rose 0.39% to 1,334.94 points, while the unweighted SASX-30 index edged up 0.96% to finish at 2,393.97 points.

The SASX-BBI, an Islamic stock index developed in partnership between SASE and Bosna Bank International (BBI), gained 0.43% to 13,285.35 points.

The SASX-Fundamentals (SASX-FN) index, tracking the top 15 companies by operating profit, rose 1.51% to end at 22,858.88 points.

The most traded share on the Sarajevo bourse this week was that of car trading company Hercegovina Auto [SAJ:HRCAR], with stock transactions totaling 3.7 million marka. The share closed at 22.3 marka on Friday, up compared with 15.37 marka at the end of the previous week.

Turnover on the Banja Luka bourse rose to 542,412 marka this week from 181,343 marka last week.

The 12-share BIRS equity index on the Banja Luka bourse went down 5% to end at 871.64 points.

Fuel trader Krajinapetrol [BANJ:KRPT-R-A] generated the highest trading turnover this week - of 421,275 marka, as its shares closed at 0.84 marka, flat compared with the end of the previous week.

The SASE operates in the Federation, whereas the BLSE operates in the Serb Republic. The Federation and the Serb Republic are Bosnia's two autonomous entities.

(1 euro = 1.956 marka)

data.title: Lost Money on Ultra Clean Holdings, Inc. (UCTT)? Join Class Action Suit Seeking Recovery - Contact Levi & Korsinsky
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / March 28, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your Ultra Clean Holdings, Inc. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/ultra-clean-holdings-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=139498&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against Ultra Clean Holdings, Inc. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between May 6, 2024 and February 24, 2025.

CASE DETAILS: According to the complaint, defendants provided investors with material information concerning the elevated demand from Chinese original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) and in the general Chinese domestic market for Ultra Clean's products throughout the fiscal year 2024. Defendants' statements included, among other things, reports of increased demand for the Company's products and services in the domestic Chinese market and reports of increased revenue, including revenue doubling with no signs of slowing down, due to the elevated demand in China for Ultra Clean's products and services.

Defendants provided these overwhelmingly positive statements to investors while, at the same time, disseminating materially false and misleading statements and/or concealing material adverse facts concerning the true state of the demand for Ultra Clean's products and services in the domestic Chinese market; notably, that the Company was facing a customer ramp issue with one of its critical customers, as well as a combination of inventory and demand corrections, which, ultimately, caused weakness for Ultra Clean in China.

On February 24, 2025, Ultra Clean published fourth quarter and full year 2024 fiscal results and hosted an associated earnings call, where the Company's executives revealed that Ultra Clean was facing "demand softness" in China. In particular, Ultra Clean was facing decreased demand in China due to extended qualification timelines and inventory absorption.

Investors and analysts reacted immediately to these revelations. The price of Ultra Clean's common stock declined dramatically. From a closing market price of $36.06 per share on February 24, 2025, Ultra Clean's stock price fell to $25.90 per share on February 25, 2025, a decline of over 28% in the span a single day.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in Ultra Clean stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/ultra-clean-holdings-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=139498&wire=1 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. There is no cost or obligation to participate.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

data.title: Descartes Systems Group Keeps Outperform Rating, US$130 Price Target at RBC Capital Markets
data.description:
The Descartes Systems Group has retained its Outperform rating and US$130 price target, RBC Capital Markets said in a Friday note after hosting Descartes' management for several investor meetings in Toronto.

RBC said the price target remains based on 30x CY26e EV/EBITDA.

RBC said it sees Descartes' valuation as attractive, given the likelihood of sustained low double-digit adjusted EBITDA growth.

While tariffs are creating uncertainties for customers, tariffs are also increasing demand for Descartes' Global Trade Intelligence solutions and are a long-term driver of supply chain transformation initiatives, RBC said.

"Descartes is conservatively managed, and we anticipate largely resilient cashflows through this environment, which we expect the company to deploy on increased M&A," RBC said.

Descartes' share price was down 1.4% at last look to $143.51.

data.title: The Real Reason Trump Pulled Stefanik's U.N. Nomination
data.description:
By Anita Hamilton

President Donald Trump is making life a little easier for Republican lawmakers looking to push through key legislation like the coming "big, beautiful" tax bill that has divided Congress members.

After announcing Thursday afternoon that Elise Stefanik, a Republican from New York, would stay in the House of Representatives instead of becoming the U.S. ambassador to the United Nations, Stefanik said she was "proud to be a team player" and was "committed to delivering results on behalf of the American people" in a Fox News interview last night.

The reality is that Republicans' majority in the House is at risk, due to a tighter-than-expected race to fill a vacant seat in Florida coming up for a vote on April 1 as well as concerns that Stefanik's seat could remain open for months, further narrowing their margin.

Republicans currently have a 218-to-213 majority in the House, which has given them little room for defection among their ranks. That narrow margin delayed passage of the stopgap spending bill, which barely passed in the House by a four-vote margin earlier in March, just days before spending was set to expire.

There are growing concerns that the Florida congressional seat vacated by Mike Waltz, who Trump tapped as his national security adviser, may not the be the easy win previously anticipated. A recent poll shows Republican Randy Fine's lead over Democrat Josh Weil in Florida's sixth district within the margin of error in the special election slated for Tuesday, April 1.

If that seat flipped to the Democrats and Stefanik's seat remained vacant until a special election is called, Republicans would have little to no room for defections. Given that some hard-line party members such as Kentucky's Thomas Massie have already balked at leadership's spending plans, moving Stefanik out of the House proved too risky.

There are currently four vacancies in the House, including Waltz's seat. The other one in Florida was vacated by Matt Gaetz, who resigned after Trump tapped him to head the Justice Department. Gaetz withdrew his nomination following a House Ethics Committee investigation into alleged sexual misconduct, which he denied. The special election for his seat is also slated for April 1.

The other two vacancies are because of the death of Democrats Sylvester Turner of Texas and Raul Grijalva of Arizona. A September vote will decide Grijalva's replacement. In Texas, Gov. Greg Abbott, a Republican, has yet to set a date for a vote on Turner's replacement in the strongly Democratic district.

Before her nomination, Stefanik was the fourth-ranking Republican in the House as chair of the House Republican Conference. However, she gave up that role after Trump nominated her to the U.N. post. House Speaker Mike Johnson said Thursday that he would invite her to "return to the leadership table immediately."

Stefanik's Senate confirmation hearing was previously stalled because Republicans needed her vote in the contentious spending bill passed earlier in March.

Write to Anita Hamilton at anita.hamilton@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.
